2a-low-porosity.title: The 2A Low-Porosity Wave Protocol
2a-low-porosity.diagnostic_summary: Your 2A waves are easily weighed down. Low porosity means products sit on the surface, making hair look greasy rather than wavy.

insightWarning.title: Weight Management Alert
insightWarning.message: Stop using heavy creams. 2A waves with low porosity require 'weightless' moisture. Switch to foams or light sprays.

upsell_tool.name: Shark FlexStyle (Diffuser)
upsell_tool.context: Hover-dry on low heat to set waves.

2a-high-porosity.title: The 2A High-Porosity Restoration Routine
2a-high-porosity.diagnostic_summary: High porosity 2A waves absorb moisture fast but lose it instantly, leading to frizz and 'limp' waves.

insightWarning.title: Cuticle Seal Alert
insightWarning.message: You must use a lightweight oil to seal the cuticle immediately after washing.

primary_cta.title: Wave Strength Protocol
primary_cta.hook: Repair the wave structure from within.

2b-low-porosity.title: The 2B Low-Porosity Definition Routine
2b-low-porosity.diagnostic_summary: 2B waves have a stronger 'S' shape but struggle with volume at the roots. Low porosity blocks deep hydration.

insightWarning.title: Root Volume Alert
insightWarning.message: Apply products away from the scalp to avoid oil buildup at the root.

primary_cta.title: The Root-Lift Blueprint
primary_cta.hook: Master the art of volume for 2B waves.

2b-high-porosity.title: The 2B High-Porosity Frizz-Proof Guide
2b-high-porosity.diagnostic_summary: High porosity makes 2B waves look frizzy and undefined. You need protein to help 'hold' the shape.

insightWarning.title: Protein Balance
insightWarning.message: Incorporate a protein-rich leave-in to give your waves more structure.

2c-low-porosity.title: The 2C Low-Porosity Coil-Prep Routine
2c-low-porosity.diagnostic_summary: 2C hair is between a wave and a curl. Low porosity makes it hard for the 'inner loop' of the wave to get hydrated.

insightWarning.title: Hydration Wall
insightWarning.message: Use a warm towel over your conditioner to 'melt' the product into the hair.

primary_cta.title: The 2C Wave-to-Curl Bridge
primary_cta.hook: Final step to turning waves into curls.

2c-high-porosity.title: The 2C High-Porosity Structure Protocol
2c-high-porosity.diagnostic_summary: Your 2C hair is fragile and prone to drying out mid-day. You need heavy sealants to lock in water.

insightWarning.title: Moisture Evaporation
insightWarning.message: If you don't seal your ends with a serum, your curls will 'vanish' by 4:00 PM.

primary_cta.title: All-Day Definition Manual
primary_cta.hook: Keep your texture looking fresh until night.

upsell_tool.name: Shark FlexStyle
upsell_tool.context: The best tool for setting 2C shapes.

3a-low-porosity.title: The 3A Low-Porosity Loop Protocol
3a-low-porosity.diagnostic_summary: 3A curls are large and loopy. Low porosity means you likely struggle with 'crunchy' hair even when wet.

insightWarning.title: The Steam Factor
insightWarning.message: You must use steam during conditioning to open your tightly closed cuticles.

primary_cta.title: The 3A Hydration Engine
primary_cta.hook: Force moisture into the core of your loops.

3a-high-porosity.title: The 3A High-Porosity Frizz-Lock Routine
3a-high-porosity.diagnostic_summary: Your 3A curls lose moisture through the 'open' scales. This leads to 'halo frizz' where the top is dry but the bottom is wet.

insightWarning.title: Halo-Frizz Warning
insightWarning.message: Apply product to soaking wet hair. Once it starts to damp-dry, the frizz is already permanent.

primary_cta.title: The 3A Moisture Sealant Guide
primary_cta.hook: Stop halo frizz before it starts.

3b-low-porosity.title: The 3B Low-Porosity Hydration Engine
3b-low-porosity.diagnostic_summary: Low porosity 3B hair has tightly closed cuticles. Moisture has a hard time getting in, but once it is there, it stays.

insightWarning.title: The 'Buildup' Alert
insightWarning.message: Heavy butters will sit on top of your hair. Use lightweight, warm applications.

3b-high-porosity.title: The 3B High-Porosity Restoration Routine
3b-high-porosity.diagnostic_summary: Your 3B pattern combined with high porosity means your hair cuticle is permanently 'open,' leading to instant frizz.

insightWarning.title: The 'Flash Drying' Trap
insightWarning.message: Stop using heavy oils first. High porosity hair requires a water-based leave-in BEFORE any sealant.

primary_cta.title: Download Your 90-Day Protocol
primary_cta.hook: The step-by-step calendar to close your cuticles.

3c-low-porosity.title: The 3C Low-Porosity Volume System
3c-low-porosity.diagnostic_summary: 3C curls are tight corkscrews. Low porosity makes the very center of the curl feel dry even after a wash.

insightWarning.title: Centrifuged Drying
insightWarning.message: Product often misses the middle of the curl. Work product in using the 'prayer hands' method for 2 minutes.

3c-high-porosity.title: The 3C High-Porosity Strengthening Path
3c-high-porosity.diagnostic_summary: Tightly packed 3C curls with high porosity are extremely prone to breakage. Your hair is naturally 'thirsty'.

insightWarning.title: Breakage Point
insightWarning.message: If you don't use a silk pillowcase, your high-porosity curls will snap simply from pillow friction.

primary_cta.title: The 3C Length Retention Guide
primary_cta.hook: Stop the breakage and actually grow length.

4a-low-porosity.title: The 4A Low-Porosity Coil Management
4a-low-porosity.diagnostic_summary: 4A coils have a visible pattern but struggle with 'stiffness' due to low porosity moisture rejection.

insightWarning.title: Stiffness Alert
insightWarning.message: Use warm water during your final rinse to keep the cuticle open as long as possible.

primary_cta.title: Soft Coil Protocol
primary_cta.hook: How to get soft, bouncy 4A coils.

4a-high-porosity.title: The 4A High-Porosity Moisture Lock
4a-high-porosity.diagnostic_summary: High porosity 4A hair is like a sponge—it absorbs everything and then dries out in 20 minutes.

insightWarning.title: Moisture Leakage
insightWarning.message: The L.O.C. (Liquid-Oil-Cream) method is non-negotiable for your profile.

primary_cta.title: L.O.C. Masterclass
primary_cta.hook: Learn to lock moisture in for days.

4b-low-porosity.title: The 4B Low-Porosity Softening Routine
4b-low-porosity.diagnostic_summary: 4B 'Z' patterns are delicate. Low porosity makes the hair feel wiry or rough to the touch.

insightWarning.title: Wiry Texture
insightWarning.message: Deep condition with heat every single week. It is the only way to soften the 4B shaft.

4b-high-porosity.title: The 4B High-Porosity Strength Protocol
4b-high-porosity.diagnostic_summary: 4B high porosity hair is the most likely to tangle into knots. You need high 'slip' and moisture retention.

insightWarning.title: Single-Strand Knots
insightWarning.message: Keep your hair stretched. High porosity 4B hair that shrinks will knot up instantly.

primary_cta.title: The Stretch & Protect System
primary_cta.hook: Eliminate knots and retain 100% of growth.

4c-low-porosity.title: The 4C Low-Porosity Growth Engine
4c-low-porosity.diagnostic_summary: 4C hair is highly fragile. Low porosity creates a barrier that makes your hair 'immune' to standard conditioners.

insightWarning.title: Conditioner Failure
insightWarning.message: Standard conditioning is useless for you without heat. Use a steamer.

primary_cta.title: The 4C Growth Engine
primary_cta.hook: Unlock growth by finally hydrating the core.

4c-high-porosity.title: The 4C High-Porosity Maximum Strength Routine
4c-high-porosity.diagnostic_summary: 4C hair is the most fragile. High porosity makes it prone to snapping because internal moisture vanishes instantly.

insightWarning.title: The 'Snap' Warning
insightWarning.message: Never detangle dry. High porosity 4C hair requires massive slip to prevent micro-tears.

primary_cta.title: The 4C Growth & Retention Bible
primary_cta.hook: Stop the breakage and actually see length.
